x-enumeration-literals.name: Liquidity Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep that consolidates funds from multiple accounts into a central account to improve liquidity management and visibility.

Comment:
Example:
A company has multiple regional accounts. At the end of the day, all excess balances are swept into a head office master account to centralize cash.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Target Balance Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep that adjusts an account balance to reach a predefined target amount.

Comment:
Example: At the end of each day, funds are moved to ensure the current account always maintains €5,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Zero Balance Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep that adjusts an account balance to zero (or a target balance) by transferring funds to or from a master account.

Comment:
Example:
A subsidiary account ends the day with €10,000. The system sweeps €10,000 to the master account, leaving the subsidiary account at €0.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Scheduled Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep executed at predefined times or intervals regardless of balance conditions.

Comment:
Example: Every Friday, €1,000 is transferred from a checking account to an investment account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Investment Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep that moves excess funds into interest-bearing or investment accounts to maximize returns.

Comment:
Example:
A current account exceeds €50,000. The excess €20,000 is automatically swept into a money market fund overnight.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Debt Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Debt Sweep (Loan/Overdraft Sweep): A sweep that uses excess funds to reduce outstanding debt or overdraft balances.

Comment:
Example:
A business account has an overdraft of €15,000.
At day-end, €5,000 excess cash is swept to reduce the overdraft to €10,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Threshold Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep triggered when account balance exceeds or falls below a defined threshold.

Comment:
Example:
If balance exceeds €100,000, the excess is swept to a savings account.
If it drops below €20,000, funds are swept back.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Notional Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Multi-Account / Notional Sweep: A sweep across multiple related accounts, often within the same organization or group.

Comment:
Example: Balances from several regional accounts are swept into a parent company account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cross-Currency Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Cross-Currency Sweep: A sweep that transfers and converts funds between accounts in different currencies.

Comment:
Example: USD balances are automatically converted and swept into a EUR account at end of day.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Reverse Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep that returns funds from a target account back to the source account when needed.

Comment
Example:
Funds were swept into an investment account.
Next day, the current account is short €3,000 → funds are swept back automatically.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Event-Driven Sweep
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A sweep triggered by a specific transaction or event.

Comment
Example:
After receiving a large customer payment, excess funds are immediately swept into an investment account.
